---
title: "Future-Ready Skills"
subtitle: "Empowering residents with future-ready skills"
author: "MJ"
date: last-modified
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Context

This chapter tracks indicators related to skills development and training across the West of England. Indicators cover metrics like qualification levels, apprenticeships, adult learning participation, skills gaps, and employment outcomes. Indicators were developed to provide high-level insights into regional trends and performance in relation to the West of England's skills priorities, which are informed by the [Growth Strategy](https://www.westofengland-ca.gov.uk/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/WE4915-Growth-Strategy-exec-summary_aw_web_v1.pdf).

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "Load required packages and source files"
#| include: false

# Load required packages
library(tidyverse)

# Source shared helpers (theme, helpers, fact_helpers, collate_fact)
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "_common.R"))

#Sourcing indicators
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4A1_no_qualifications.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4A2_level4_plus.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4A3_apprenticeship_starts.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4A4_ks4_attainment8.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4B1_employment_rate_16_64.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4B2_economic_inactivity.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4B3_neet_16_17.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4B4_neet_18_24.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4B5_below_living_wage.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4B6_claimant_deprived_wards.R"))
source(here::here("scripts", "R", "04-skills", "RI_4C1_skills_gaps.R"))

indicator_fact_tbl <- collate_fact() |>
  build_reporting_view()
```

```{r}
#| label: priority_summary
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: Priority 4 indicator summary table
format_priority_summary(
  indicator_fact_tbl,
  core_dim_data_tbl,
  priority = 4,
  title = "Future-Ready Skills: Indicator Summary"
)
```

All indicators in this section relate to the wider West of England geography, which includes Bath and North East Somerset, Bristol, North Somerset and South Gloucestershire. This geography extends beyond the West of England Combined Authority area to include North Somerset.
---
## Residents with No Qualifications

```{r}
#| label: RI_4A1_no_qualifications_gt
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4A1_no_qualifications summary"

indicator_fact_tbl |>
  format_indicator_summary(
    "RI_4A1_no_qualifications",
    title = "Residents with No Qualifications",
    subtitle = "Working-age population with no formal qualifications",
    units = "%"
  )
```

### Overview

Qualifications are a key driver of employment, productivity and economic opportunity. This indicator measures the proportion of working-age residents with no formal qualifications. Lower levels indicate a more skilled workforce and greater access to employment opportunities. Reducing the number of residents without qualifications supports the West of England Growth Strategy's ambition to equip people with the skills needed to access future jobs, improve productivity and ensure the benefits of economic growth are shared across communities.

```{r}
#| label: RI_4A1_no_qualifications_plot
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4A1_no_qualifications line chart"

RI_4A1_no_qualifications_plot
```


## Residents with Level 4+ Qualifications

```{r}
#| label: RI_4A2_level4_plus_gt
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4A2_level4_plus summary"

indicator_fact_tbl |>
  format_indicator_summary(
    "RI_4A2_level4_plus",
    title = "Residents with Level 4+ Qualifications",
    subtitle = "Working-age population qualified to Level 4 and above",
    units = "%"
  )
```

### Overview

Higher-level qualifications are an important indicator of workforce capability and economic competitiveness. This indicator measures the proportion of working-age residents qualified to Level 4 and above. (Level 3 qualifications include A levels and equivalent qualifications, this indicator captures qualifications above this level). These include a range of higher education qualifications, from Higher National Certificates and Certificates of Higher Education through to bachelor's degrees (Level 6) and postgraduate qualifications. Higher levels of attainment support innovation, productivity and growth in knowledge intensive sectors. The indicator reflects the region's ability to develop and attract the talent needed to support future economic growth.

```{r}
#| label: RI_4A2_level4_plus_plot
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4A2_level4_plus line chart"

RI_4A2_level4_plus_plot
```


## Apprenticeship Starts

```{r}
#| label: RI_4A3_apprenticeship_starts_gt
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4A3_apprenticeship_starts summary"

indicator_fact_tbl |>
  format_indicator_summary(
    "RI_4A3_apprenticeship_starts",
    title = "Apprenticeship Starts",
    subtitle = "Apprenticeship starts across the region",
    units = "Starts"
  )
```

### Overview

Apprenticeships provide an important route into skilled employment and career progression. This indicator measures participation in apprenticeship programmes across the region. Strong apprenticeship uptake helps employers develop the workforce they need while providing residents with opportunities to gain qualifications and practical experience.

```{r}
#| label: RI_4A3_apprenticeship_starts_plot
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4A3_apprenticeship_starts line chart"

RI_4A3_apprenticeship_starts_plot
```


## KS4 Attainment 8

```{r}
#| label: RI_4A4_ks4_attainment8_gt
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4A4_ks4_attainment8 summary"

indicator_fact_tbl |>
  format_indicator_summary(
    "RI_4A4_ks4_attainment8",
    title = "KS4 Attainment 8",
    subtitle = "Average Attainment 8 score at Key Stage 4",
    units = "Score"
  )
```

### Overview

Educational attainment provides the foundation for future learning, skills development and employment opportunities. This indicator measures average pupil attainment across a range of subjects at the end of Key Stage 4 (GCSE). Strong educational outcomes help ensure young people are equipped to progress into further education, training and employment. Comparisons over time should be interpreted with some caution, as educational attainment and assessment arrangements were affected by the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly during 2020 and 2021. 

```{r}
#| label: RI_4A4_ks4_attainment8_plot
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4A4_ks4_attainment8 line chart"

RI_4A4_ks4_attainment8_plot
```


## Employment Rate 16-64

```{r}
#| label: RI_4B1_employment_rate_16_64_gt
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4B1_employment_rate_16_64 summary"

indicator_fact_tbl |>
  format_indicator_summary(
    "RI_4B1_employment_rate_16_64",
    title = "Employment Rate 16-64",
    subtitle = "Proportion of working-age residents in employment",
    units = "%"
  )
```

### Overview

Employment is a key measure of economic participation and prosperity. This indicator measures the proportion of working-age residents who are in employment. Higher employment rates indicate that residents are benefiting from economic opportunities and contributing their skills within the labour market. Sustaining high employment levels is important for supporting inclusive growth and regional productivity.

```{r}
#| label: RI_4B1_employment_rate_16_64_plot
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4B1_employment_rate_16_64 line chart"

RI_4B1_employment_rate_16_64_plot
```


## Economic Inactivity

```{r}
#| label: RI_4B2_economic_inactivity_gt
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4B2_economic_inactivity summary"

indicator_fact_tbl |>
  format_indicator_summary(
    "RI_4B2_economic_inactivity",
    title = "Economic Inactivity",
    subtitle = "Proportion of working-age residents economically inactive",
    units = "%"
  )
```

### Overview

Economic inactivity can limit workforce participation and reduce the availability of skills within the labour market. This indicator measures the proportion of working-age residents who are neither employed or actively seeking work. Lower levels of inactivity indicate greater participation in economic activity and access to employment opportunities. Reducing inactivity is an important part of addressing labour shortages and supporting inclusive growth.

```{r}
#| label: RI_4B2_economic_inactivity_plot
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4B2_economic_inactivity line chart"

RI_4B2_economic_inactivity_plot
```


## NEET 16-17

```{r}
#| label: RI_4B3_neet_16_17_gt
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4B3_neet_16_17 summary"

indicator_fact_tbl |>
  format_indicator_summary(
    "RI_4B3_neet_16_17",
    title = "NEET 16-17",
    subtitle = "16-17 year olds not in education, employment or training",
    units = "%"
  )
```

### Overview

Participation in education, employment or training during the transition from school is important for long-term outcomes. This indicator measures the proportion of 16-17 year olds who are not in education, employment or training (NEET). Lower rates indicate that more young people are progressing into positive destinations and developing the skills needed for future careers. Reducing NEET levels helps strengthen the future workforce and improve social mobility.

```{r}
#| label: RI_4B3_neet_16_17_plot
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4B3_neet_16_17 line chart"

RI_4B3_neet_16_17_plot
```


## NEET 18-24
Please note: This indicator is currently under development and data is not yet available for reporting. Analysis will be included in a future update of the Regional Indicators report.

## Below Living Wage

```{r}
#| label: RI_4B5_below_living_wage_gt
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4B5_below_living_wage summary"

indicator_fact_tbl |>
  format_indicator_summary(
    "RI_4B5_below_living_wage",
    title = "Below Living Wage",
    subtitle = "Proportion of employees earning below the Living Wage",
    units = "%"
  )
```

### Overview

The quality of employment is an important aspect of economic wellbeing. This indicator measures the proportion of employees earning below the Real Living Wage, as defined by the Living Wage Foundation. Lower levels indicate that a greater share of workers are earning wages that better reflect the cost of living. Monitoring this indicator helps assess whether economic growth is translating into improved living standards and the creation of quality employment opportunities. The most recent year of data is provisional and should be interpreted with caution, as revised estimates will be published the following year.

```{r}
#| label: RI_4B5_below_living_wage_plot
#| eval: true
#| code-summary: "RI_4B5_below_living_wage line chart"

RI_4B5_below_living_wage_plot
```


## Claimant Count in Deprived Wards
Please note: This indicator is currently under development and data is not yet available for reporting. Analysis will be included in a future update of the Regional Indicators report.


## Skills Gaps

Please note: This indicator is currently under development and data is not yet available for reporting. Analysis will be included in a future update of the Regional Indicators report.
